content.problemStatement:
You are given a grid with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM). Let's denote the cell in row rrr and column ccc by (r,c)(r, c)(r,c). Two cells of the grid are adjacent if they share a side.

Some of the cells of this grid contain treasures. You do not know exactly which cells contain them, but an analysis of the grid, called a treasure hunt map, is available. For each cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j), you are given an integer Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j‚Äč with the following meaning:


Ai,j=‚ąí1A_{i, j} = -1Ai,j‚Äč=‚ąí1: no information
Ai,j=0A_{i, j} = 0Ai,j‚Äč=0: there is an even number of cells containing a treasure which are adjacent to the cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j)
Ai,j=1A_{i, j} = 1Ai,j‚Äč=1: there is an odd number of cells containing a treasure which are adjacent to the cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j)
A treasure layout is the set of all cells containing treasures. Find the number of possible treasure layouts that are consistent with all the given information. Since the answer may be large, compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: ‚ą£Ai,j‚ą£‚Č§1|A_{i, j}| \le 1‚ą£Ai,j‚Äč‚ą£‚Č§1 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), the iii-th of these lines contains MMM space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,‚Ä¶,Ai,MA_{i, 1}, A_{i, 2}, \ldots, A_{i, M}Ai,1‚Äč,Ai,2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ai,M‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the number of treasure layouts modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
